test.name: KEYWORDS for bigram
test.db_create: CREATE TABLE `test_table` ( `id` int(11) NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT, `gid` int(11) NOT NULL, `title` varchar(255) NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (`id`) )
test.db_insert: INSERT INTO `test_table` VALUES ( 1, 1, 'manticore search is the best'), (2, 2, 'manticore is more better' ), (3, 3, 'search with manticore like many' )
test.db_create: CREATE TABLE `test_table2` ( `id` int(11) NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT, `gid` int(11) NOT NULL, `title` varchar(255) NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (`id`) )
test.db_insert: INSERT INTO `test_table2` VALUES ( 1, 1, 'intel i3 10200'), (2, 2, 'intel i5 10500' ), (3, 3, 'intel pentium' )
test.db_create: CREATE TABLE `test_table3` ( `id` int(11) NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT, `gid` int(11) NOT NULL, `title` varchar(255) NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (`id`) )
test.db_insert: INSERT INTO `test_table3` VALUES (1, 1, 'xt 806'), (2, 2, 'xt rt9600'), (3, 3, 'xt abc'), (4, 4, 'xt v2')

queries.sphinxql: CALL KEYWORDS ( '*intel*', 'idx_bi2', 1 AS stats, 'hits' AS sort_mode )
CALL KEYWORDS ( '*intel*', 'rt_bi2', 1 AS stats, 'hits' AS sort_mode )
